title: Ideas of what Adam and Lilith look like naturally
display_title: Ideas of what Adam and Lilith look like naturally
wikitext:
[]: Ideas of what Adam & Lilith look like naturally.             Quote Post by DanYeomans » Sat Aug 06, 2011 1:09 am We never really get to see either Adam or Lilith in their natural forms. We see 3 images of Adam: an embryo, a snow-covered humanoid body, and an Eva-like "giant of light". As for Lilith, we see 2 forms: A large Rei, and a white, fleshy humanoid mass bound to a cross. We can't really assume that Adam looks like Kaworu or that Lilith looks like Rei simply because those are the Human representations of these characters. We can assume that some traits are shared between the two, but that's as far as it would go. So, what is everyone else's opinions on this?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 06, 2011 3:39 pm This is assuming they have a natural form to begin with, given that they can look like anything they want. Since Lilith's form is pretty much what NERV found her in, and they found Adam in a similar shape, the two probably look like that by default.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 9:35 am Both Adam and Lilith probably looked like pale, Eva-like giants at the start. (The Angels seem to be the same thing, on a genetic level.) Cloning Lilith produces something that looks like an Adam clone, which means that -- unless Eva-01 is really a clone of Adam after all -- Lilith's fat, putty-ish body isn't her "true form". Discrepancies between the "Adam excavation" image and "Giant of Light" can probably be chalked up to: - the addition of pylons and other doodads by the KatsExp team - lack of continuity with regard to body proportions (this problem also affects the Evas throughout the show) - the whole glowing thing

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Aug 07, 2011 10:00 am Reichu wrote: Both Adam and Lilith probably looked like pale, Eva-like giants at the start. (The Angels seem to be the same thing, on a genetic level.) Cloning Lilith produces something that looks like an Adam clone, which means that -- unless Eva-01 is really a clone of Adam after all -- Lilith's fat, putty-ish body isn't her "true form". Discrepancies between the "Adam excavation" image and "Giant of Light" can probably be chalked up to: - the addition of pylons and other doodads by the KatsExp team - lack of continuity with regard to body proportions (this problem also affects the Evas throughout the show) - the whole glowing thing

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 11:54 am CJD wrote: Isn't there something in the Kaworu ending that describes how the "Seeds" were chosen from their (FAR) species? So, wouldn't Adam & Lilith's "True" form be that of the FAR?

[]: Re: Ideas of what Adam & Lilith look like naturally.             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 07, 2011 11:55 am DanYeomans wrote: We see 3 images of Adam: an embryo, a snow-covered humanoid body , and an Eva-like "giant of light".

[]: Re: Ideas of what Adam & Lilith look like naturally.             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 07, 2011 11:58 am TehDonutKing wrote: When?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 11:59 am This image used to be tucked away in the 23 (video version) Reiquarium scene, but they took it out of the Renewal version for no obvious reason. (...does it snow in subterranean caverns?)

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Aug 07, 2011 12:07 pm Reichu wrote: This image used to be tucked away in the 23 (video version) Reiquarium scene, but they took it out of the Renewal version for no obvious reason.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 07, 2011 3:10 pm Easy. They looked like this:

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Sun Aug 07, 2011 3:47 pm Reichu wrote: (...does it snow in subterranean caverns?)

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Sun Aug 07, 2011 4:38 pm I imagine that Lilith and Adam both looked very similar initially, but years of loneliness, trashy romance novels and Häagen-Dazs left Lil the blubbery, perpetually menstruating mess that she is in the series. Adam was no doubt the athletic over-achiever who their parents both loved more.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 07, 2011 5:12 pm Reichu wrote: Cloning Lilith produces something that looks like an Adam clone, which means that -- unless Eva-01 is really a clone of Adam after all -- Lilith's fat, putty-ish body isn't her "true form".

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Aug 07, 2011 5:17 pm NAveryW wrote: Easy. They looked like this:

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 07, 2011 6:18 pm It rarely snows in Antarctica. It's a desert.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 6:21 pm Bagheera: It seems like you're taking the path of greatest resistance in order to explain the acute physical similarities between Eva-01, the other Evas, and the Giant of Light. the different forms of Adam in 21'.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 07, 2011 6:50 pm Reichu wrote: Bagheera: It seems like you're taking the path of greatest resistance in order to explain the acute physical similarities between Eva-01, the other Evas, and the Giant of Light.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 7:00 pm Bagheera wrote: Not really. The giant of light looks different from snow/ice-covered Adam in much the same way the Evas look different from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 07, 2011 7:09 pm Reichu wrote: As I said earlier, the proportions of the giants (and their degree of muscular definition, etc.) in the show are far from constant... Does a physical change happen every time one of these inconsistencies occur? Why should we conclude that Adam or Eva-01('s upper half) looking more human-like in the 'mystery images' means that something happened to them to turn them into elongated, anorexic waifs?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 7:14 pm Bagheera wrote: By that logic your rationale for concluding that what we see in TD isn't Lilith's true form goes out the window.
